issue_title: torch.logdet produces incorrect results for singular matrices on CUDA vs CPU
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `high priority`. Maintainer `high priority` label confirms the bug is important and acknowledged.

linked_prs.title: Fix logdet returning finite values for singular matrices on CUDA
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: PR was merged briefly then immediately reverted. COLLABORATOR soumith requested merge three times; pytorchmergebot merged it on the third attempt on 2025-07-10. A revert was requested the next day (2025-07-12) by huydhn (CONTRIBUTOR) because it broke internal Facebook linear_operator tests (inv_quad_logdet accuracy failures). pytorchmergebot confirmed the revert succeeded. The PR approach (two-tier singularity detection with epsilon threshold) was too aggressive and introduced false positives. A better fix is needed — the root cause and general approach are understood but the threshold-based detection must be made more robust.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR lezcano submitted APPROVED review on 2025-07-10 with 'SGTM'. COLLABORATOR IvanYashchuk submitted APPROVED review on 2025-07-10. COLLABORATOR albanD submitted APPROVED review on 2025-07-10. COLLABORATOR soumith wrote '@pytorchbot merge' three times. PR was merged, then reverted due to internal accuracy test failures in linear_operator library.

maintainer_signals.author: IvanYashchuk
maintainer_signals.quote: A better non-synchronizing solution could include a `torch.where` call to replace all small numbers, smaller than a certain epsilon value, with 0.0

confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: COLLABORATOR approved_fix signals (lezcano, IvanYashchuk, albanD all approved PR #157910) → +0.20. Running total: 0.95. Step 3: COLLABORATOR soumith wrote '@pytorchbot merge' (requested_merge) → +0.20. Running total: 1.15. Step 4: Repro script present — exact 7-line Python snippet in issue body demonstrating CPU returns -inf, GPU returns -15.13 for a singular matrix → +0.15. Running total: 1.30. Step 5: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.40. Step 6: fix_scope is 'single_file' (LinearAlgebra.cpp) → +0.10. Running total: 1.50. Step 7: 'confirmed' reproducibility — user jiren-the-gray confirmed still reproducible in torch 2.9.0 on 2025-12-30 → no negative adjustment. Step 8: The linked PR was reverted after merging due to internal accuracy test failures — the approach (epsilon threshold singularity detection) introduced false positives. This is not a hard 'pr_rejected' blocker per the schema since a maintainer subsequently commented 'https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157910 fixes this issue' and the PR had COLLABORATOR approval. However, the revert and known accuracy issues suggest the fix needs a more careful approach. Apply -0.10 for the extra complexity of needing a revised approach beyond PR #157910. Running total: 1.40. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0]: 1.00. Final: applying -0.10 for reverted prior attempt requiring new approach → 0.90.
ground_truth_delta: Very similar to positive_71774: both are silent correctness bugs on a specific dtype/device combination with a COLLABORATOR-approved fix PR that was not successfully merged. Key differences: (1) Issue #154312 is more recent (2025 vs 2022) so staleness is not a concern. (2) The fix attempt for #154312 was actually merged and then reverted due to breaking internal accuracy tests — the revert clarifies that the epsilon threshold approach is wrong, giving a clear signal for what a revised fix must avoid. (3) The root cause in #154312 is better understood (cuSOLVER produces tiny non-zero diagonals ~1e-16 for singular matrices) and multiple COLLABORATORs (lezcano, IvanYashchuk, albanD) engaged actively with the PR. This makes #154312 more actionable than positive_71774 — a contributor can study PR #157910's approach, understand why the revert happened, and file a corrected fix.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #157910 ('Fix logdet returning finite values for singular matrices on CUDA') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size. The PR received a COLLABORATOR APPROVED review — the fix approach is already validated.

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #154312. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: Re-apply the fix from PR #157910, which received 'SGTM' from lezcano and two APPROVED reviews before being merged — then immediately reverted due to a CI flaky test. The fix approach is sound; the new PR must also fix or skip the flaky test that triggered the revert.
contribution_path.warning: The fix was reverted on the same day it merged due to CI failures — identify which test flaked (likely in test/test_linalg.py) and mark it xfail or fix the numerical tolerance before resubmitting.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_linalg.py -k logdet`. Find the flaky test that caused the revert of PR #157910 and fix or stabilize it before submitting the new PR.
contribution_path.warning: The previous fix was reverted due to a flaky CUDA test — do not resubmit without confirming the test suite is stable across multiple runs.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #154312' and 'Revives #157910' in the body. Tag @lezcano @IvanYashchuk @soumith for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
contribution_path.warning: Note in the PR body that the previous fix (PR #157910) was reverted for CI flakiness, and explain how you resolved that issue.
